cards.question: Page 9 in 'Chapter 1: Pages 1-25' (General section) - detail 1: Explain 'Course overview Course description and agenda' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Course overview Course description and agenda Infor M3 Ad hoc reporting is a tool that enables the creation of operational reports and listings direct from the Infor M3 application database. Ad hoc reporting further reduces the need to modify or invest in developing replacements for standard Infor M3 operational reports in order to personalize the output from Infor M3. In addition, it also avoids the inappropriate use of advanced business intelligence or anal ytic tools to satisfy relatively simple operational needs and manages access and security using standard Infor M3 user security and privilege levels. With the Ad hoc reporting tool, Infor M3 users can manage the design, development, storage, execution, and scheduling of user-defined reports. All output is delivered in standard extensible markup language (XML) or comma-separated values (CSV) format to allow choice and flexibility in its end use. This training is for M3 Cloud Edition

cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 1: Introduction to Infor M3 Ad hoc reporting Overview An organization’s reporting needs can vary greatly. What is most important is to get the information you need when you need it. With the M3 Ad hoc reporting tools, Infor M3 super users and IT support personnel can create and maintain flexible user-defined reports and lists based on the M3 Business Engine database. The operational reports and listings created with the tool allow users to generate reports in an XML or CSV format to be consumed by products such as Infor Document Management (IDM). The custom design allows users to add related tables and virtual fields, and the ability to restrict the report to certain security groups. cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 1: Introduction to Infor M3 Ad hoc reporting About Infor M3 Ad hoc reporting Infor M3 Ad hoc reporting is a tool within the M3 Business Engine that enables the building of your own reports over any Infor M3 standard table. In the Cloud Edition (CE), users may select how to configure their ad hoc report using one of the design tools. An ad hoc report group is defined for a specific master table that determines the sorting possibilities. You can connect multiple reports to any ad hoc report group. When creating a report, and when setting up your report group, there are two design tools available: • = Ad hoc report designer • = Configurable XML For both options, a report can contain a unique set of filters and selection parameters. A batch job processes an Ad Hoc report generation and creates the output. The batch job can be triggered by the user or ran through the Infor M3 job scheduler. The output can be in XML or CSV format. Open The Document loc record holds a method and location path that are used to generate a document/file destination. The location path based on the indicated method is included in a produced XML file as information. It is up to the application that processes the XML file (for example, StreamServe) to use the information. cards.goodAnswer: Lesson 2: Configuring report settings Report creation process flow The ad hoc development workflow starts with a report group. Ad hoc reports are based on a master table (e.g., Customer Order [CO] line [OOLINE]). Reports that use the same master table are grouped together in a report group listed in the Ad Hoc Report Group. Open (AHS100) program. Report development workflow When creating an ad hoc report, you must first determine the master table and then find out if a group already exists. If a group does exist, you can add your report to that group, if not, you need to create a new one. From this level all the connected information can be created or maintained; all changes will affect any ad hoc report connected to the selected group. The Infor provided report groups in Ad hoc reporting use a naming convention consisting of M3_ and the master table name. Aggregation levels are selected starting from the first key field and then field by field throughout the sorting order definition. How information is presented in aggregated records is defined per view using the Aggregation display field. You can define an unlimited number of virtual fields. When created, the virtual fields can be selected as fields (columns) in the list (report). Examples of this type of virtual field include Display % (&%%%%) and Margin Text (&MART). 7-Date Virtual date fields may convert a date to a unique sequence number. That sequence number can in turn by used in Calculated virtual fields. For example, by using two virtual date fields, you can calculate the difference between the two dates. The first day in the system calendar is assigned a sequence number of 1, and each day after is assigned a subsequent number. You can also indicate if sequencing should include all days, only working days, or only bank days. Examples of this type of virtual field include Current Date (&CUDT) and Requested Date (&RLDT). The file is generated by the Infor M3 BE and processed for import built in import functionality and application programming interfaces (APIs). For the process to be successful, the XML file must contain three sections, as described in the table below. XML section Description Check This optional section includes API programs and transactions that verify related data is valid before processing of Delete and Add transactions. If any error message is received from the API transactions, the process will end the process, and the Add and Delete sections will not be processed. Delete This mandatory section specifies API programs/transactions that delete existing data. This section is only processed if the Replace existing data check box is selected.
